text.title: Paid Ads Creative Library Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Paid Ads Creative Library application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Every creative is tagged: format (video/static/carousel), hook type, value prop, audience target, platform. Untagged = invisible to next iteration.
rule.rationale: Without tags, library is a dump; with tags, it's a queryable corpus that fuels iteration.

rule.statement: Treat creatives like code: versioned (v1.0, v1.1), changelog per version, parent → child lineage. Failed versions kept for reference.
rule.rationale: Version control prevents 're-discovering' bad creatives + enables A/B history.

rule.statement: Each asset has performance row: CPM, CTR, CVR, spend, lifetime. Updated weekly. Drives next-iteration prompts.
rule.rationale: Without performance feedback, library decays into inventory; performance row is the learning signal.

rule.statement: AI-generated variants allowed but tagged 'AI-generated'; quality gate (≥1 human review) before activation; brand-tone check.
rule.rationale: AI volume without quality gate = brand drift + policy violations; explicit gate keeps quality.

rule.statement: Quarterly: archive assets with zero activity in 6 months AND inferior performance vs current top quartile. Library bloat = retrieval friction.
rule.rationale: Without pruning, library grows to thousands; retrieval friction kills iteration speed.
